description_long: This object is a boxy, industrial-style air conditioner unit with a weathered white metal exterior, showing visible rust stains along the upper edges. The front face features a large, circular fan grille with a dense radial pattern, designed for efficient airflow. The sides and top are plain, with subtle panel lines and additional rust marks, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The rear view reveals the interior of the unit, with the fan visible through an open panel, and more pronounced rust streaks on the bottom surface. The overall construction suggests durability and functionality, typical of units found in urban environments such as alleys, rooftops, or building exteriors. The lack of visible controls or branding emphasizes its utilitarian design.
description: A weathered, box-shaped air conditioner with a large circular fan grille on the front and rust-stained white metal panels.
description_view_0: A plain, rust-stained white metal panel with subtle seams, showing the side of the air conditioner.
description_view_1: The front view displays a prominent circular fan grille with a dense radial pattern and rust along the top edge.
description_view_2: Another plain side panel, similar to view 0, with visible rust and faint panel lines.
description_view_3: The rear view reveals the interior with the fan visible through an open panel and heavy rust streaks on the bottom.
attribute_grille_pattern: Dense circular radial grille with thin, evenly spaced spokes radiating from the center.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Single large circular vent on the front with a radial grille; rear is open for airflow, no additional vents visible.
